summary:
For an alcoholic presenting with abdominal pain and vomiting but having a negative chest X-ray, prioritize measuring serum lipase as it is sensitive for detecting pancreatic inflammation due to alcohol use; consider additional tests if initial findings are inconclusive after reviewing these laboratory results. To directly address the requirements:

For a chronically alcoholic patient with epigastric pain and vomiting who has no abnormality seen on his chest X-ray, measure serum lipase first to check for possible alcoholic pancreatitis, which can explain his symptoms effectively through blood testing prior to any invasive procedures unless indicated otherwise based on result interpretation.
